name: TAO Session Drive Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: opening-range or session-window continuation when a specific session structurally dominates follow-through
thesis: TAO 4h is the best primary lane for testing whether a dominant session push can continue beyond the initial impulse when price holds above reclaimed fast value instead of snapping back into balance. The edge is continuation after session dominance proves real, not blind breakout chasing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if session-drive holds do not generate enough trades, if follow-through repeatedly dies after the first bar, or if the move is just noisy expansion without durable directional persistence.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A dominant session or range window sets directional control, price holds above reclaimed fast value after the drive, and then produces a second continuation leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In mild trend-up conditions, directional participation can persist beyond the first impulse if a session establishes control and price holds above value.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is suitable because session-driven pushes are visible at this horizon and the asset expresses continuation strongly when the move is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The session impulse may be real but too short-lived, causing entries after the hold to arrive after most of the move is spent.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but travel is shallow, de-risk faster and shorten holding time before changing the core continuation grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A workable number of continuation entries with expectancy concentrated in the first target and only occasional runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h plus one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after the session-drive hold.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or repeated holds that reverse back into balance immediately.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; drive, hold, and persistence are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad post-drive hold logic rather than exact one-bar triggers

validation_basket.why_included: Best expressive lane for visible session-drive behavior in the live basket.
validation_basket.continue_if: Hold-and-go continuation trades with usable density and positive follow-through after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The hold confirms too rarely or follow-through dies immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Repeated directional session pushes followed by a tradable continuation leg.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows the same session-drive hold shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The pattern only appears on TAO-style behavior.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner continuation holds than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism can survive on another explosive lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS preserves the hold-and-go pattern without collapsing into chaos.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS becomes too violent for reliable continuation entries.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Larger but noisier continuation bursts than TAO.
